name: Material Design 3
description: Google's Material Design 3 tonal-palette system: every key color becomes a 13-tone ramp in HCT space, and the whole scheme is regenerated algorithmically from a single seed color (#6750A4 for the baseline) by @material/material-color-utilities.
philosophy: Each key color is a tonal palette: constant CAM16 hue and chroma, sampled at 13 canonical tones of CIE L* lightness (0 = black, 100 = white) in the HCT color space. There is one palette per color for both appearances — dark mode is tone re-selection, not a second palette: light scheme picks tone 40 for solid/primary, 90 for containers, 98 for surface, 10 for text; dark scheme picks tone 80 for primary, 30 for containers, 6 for surface, 90 for text. Hover and pressed states are opacity state layers over the resolved role color, not separate tones.
solidForeground: white in light scheme (on-primary = tone 100); dark scheme inverts: solid becomes tone 80 with a tone-20 dark foreground
notes: Steps are listed light-to-dark (tone 100 first, tone 0 last) to match the other systems in this playground; Material's own tone numbers run the opposite way (0 = black, 100 = white). M3 is single-palette by design: light and dark schemes select different tones from the same 13-tone palettes (e.g. surface N98 light vs N6 dark, primary 40 vs 80, on-surface N10 vs N90), so dark is null on every step; the roleMapping reflects the light scheme. Role-mapping basis (material-web v0.192 _md-sys-color.scss light scheme): surface=N98 (nearest canonical tone 99), surface-container-low=N96 (nearest 95), primary-container/error-container=tone 90, outline-variant=NV80, outline=NV50, primary/error=tone 40, on-surface-variant=NV30, on-surface=N10; hover/pressed and a third border weight have no tone — M3 uses opacity state layers — so those roles are null. The baseline SCSS additionally defines non-canonical surface tones on Neutral only (4, 6, 12, 17, 22, 24, 87, 92, 94, 96, 98) used by the surface-container roles; they are omitted here to keep steps uniform across scales. Values come verbatim from material-web v0.192 tokens except Green and Amber: the M3 baseline defines no success/warning palettes, so those two were generated by running the official @material/material-color-utilities@0.4.0 (TonalPalette.fromInt, no harmonization) on the classic 2014 Material palette seeds Green 500 #4CAF50 and Amber 500 #FFC107 (seeds fetched from Flutter's material/colors.dart) — exactly M3's documented custom-color workflow. Caveat: MCU 0.4.0 regenerated from seed #6750A4 drifts slightly from the shipped v0.192 baseline (e.g. primary10 #22005d vs #21005d; error40 #ba1a1a vs #b3261e), so the static tokens were preferred where they exist. Hover/active states are alpha state layers (8%/12% overlays), not palette steps. The m3.material.io and m2.material.io pages were fetched but are JS-rendered shells; machine-readable equivalents (material-web token SCSS, Flutter colors.dart) were used instead.
